jsonExactnessPolicy.rawJsonParsingPrecondition: detect duplicate member names in raw JSON text before converting to an object

templates.synthetic: Schematic — illustrative synthetic data, not measured.
templates.advisory_only: Advisory — advisory evidence only; not a calibrated posterior.
templates.not_paper_local: Advisory — not paper-local evidence; candidate ranking only.

honestyPolicy.callerCaptionControls: escape C0/C1, bidi, zero-width, and BOM controls

evaluationOrder: parse/coerce every JSON number to IEEE-754 binary64
validate and snapshot the raw envelope with exact-JSON budgets
validate skill params, provenance values, and portable constraints
derive and display the mandatory honesty caption
validate every present known provenance value

semantics.equals_param: declared value must equal one checked top-level params property under Object.is
semantics.equals_param_path: declared value must equal the checked scalar reached through a dot-separated sequence of safe own data-property names under Object.is
semantics.equals_literal: declared value must equal the contract literal under Object.is

strictProvenancePolicy.presentKnownValues: validate every present known key with provenanceValueConstraints
strictProvenancePolicy.requiredKeysControl: presence only; value rules apply whether required or extra

strictInvocationPolicy.externalSelection: validateSkillInvocation(id,payload): explicit id selects; payload.skill is optional but must match when present
strictInvocationPolicy.selfDescribingSelection: validateSpec(payload): payload.skill is required and selects the contract
strictInvocationPolicy.hostSelection: host envelopes require payload.skill; explicit id and payload.skill must match
strictInvocationPolicy.cortexelEnvelope: allowed iff contract.scene is non-null; payload.scene must equal contract.scene
strictInvocationPolicy.hostEnvelope: allowed iff contract.scene is null; scene is forbidden
strictInvocationPolicy.rendererRoute: when selected, must occur in contract.rendererRoutes
strictInvocationPolicy.provenance: apply strictProvenancePolicy, require every contract.requiredProvenanceFlags value, then evaluate every provenanceParamConstraint

stringNormalizationPolicy.displayStringControls: reject C0/C1, bidi, zero-width, and BOM controls

jsonBudgetSemantics.scope: one snapshot of the complete invocation envelope
jsonBudgetSemantics.nodeCount: every scalar, array, and object value; property names are not nodes
jsonBudgetSemantics.totalStringLength: all string values plus every object property name
jsonBudgetSemantics.repeatedReference: counted once per JSON occurrence; cycles reject

paletteRegistryPolicy.validation: selected palette name must exist in the active runtime registry
paletteRegistryPolicy.registration: strict descriptor snapshot, validated then frozen

equal_length.rule: all present arrays have identical length

each_length_matches.pathRoles: first path resolves zero or more arrays; last path is the reference array
each_length_matches.rule: every first-path array length equals the reference-array length

monotonic_non_decreasing.pathRoles: each path resolves an ordered numeric sequence
monotonic_non_decreasing.rule: for every adjacent pair previous <= next

property_count.rule: own enumerable property count is within optional min/max inclusive

unique_field.pathRoles: the first path resolves an array of objects; field names the key
unique_field.rule: field values are unique under JSON scalar equality

unique_tuple.pathRoles: paths resolve equal-length scalar sequences zipped by index
unique_tuple.rule: zipped JSON tuples are unique; when the final kind value is in symmetricKinds, canonicalize the first two tuple values lexicographically

references_exist.pathRoles: all paths except the last resolve references; the last resolves the allowed-id set
references_exist.rule: every non-null reference occurs in the allowed-id set

no_self_loops.pathRoles: first and second paths resolve equal-length source and target sequences

same_keys.rule: all objects have exactly the same own enumerable string-key set

cartesian_product_length.pathRoles: first path resolves axis arrays; second path resolves output arrays
cartesian_product_length.rule: every output-array length equals the product of all axis-array lengths

permutation_of_keys.pathRoles: first path resolves a scalar sequence; second path resolves an object
permutation_of_keys.rule: the sequence contains every object key exactly once

endpoint_kinds.pathRoles: first path resolves edges with source/target/kind; second resolves nodes with id/kind
endpoint_kinds.rule: each edge endpoint node kind equals allowedEndpointKinds[edge.kind]

mapped_value.pathRoles: first path resolves a discriminator scalar; second path resolves its dependent scalar
mapped_value.rule: the second value equals allowedValues[first value]

conditional_numeric_domain.pathRoles: first path resolves a discriminator scalar; second path resolves numeric values
conditional_numeric_domain.rule: every numeric value satisfies numericDomains[discriminator] inclusive min/max and optional integer requirement

uniform_histogram_bins.pathRoles: first path resolves the ordered bin-center array; second path resolves one numeric bin width
uniform_histogram_bins.rule: width is positive and finite; centers are strictly increasing; each adjacent delta approximately equals width
uniform_histogram_bins.nonNegativeLowerEdge: when true, firstCenter-width/2 must be >= -tolerance, where tolerance uses firstCenter and width/2 in the same comparison formula

normalized_histogram_mass.pathRoles: first path resolves normalization mode; second resolves histogram values; third resolves bin width
normalized_histogram_mass.absentMode: when normalizationRules has no entry for the selected mode, skip the constraint
normalized_histogram_mass.accumulation: values must be finite and non-negative and are summed from index 0 to length-1 using IEEE-754 binary64 addition

measures.sum: compare the left-to-right value sum with target
measures.density_integral: multiply the left-to-right value sum by the positive finite width, then compare with target

psth_derived_counts.pathRoles: normalization mode, values array, positive safe-integer trial count, positive finite bin width in ms, and aggregation literal in that order
psth_derived_counts.aggregation: selected_senders_per_trial means each bin count is the aggregate number of raw spike events from all selected senders across the declared trials
psth_derived_counts.operationOrder: evaluate the displayed rate_hz expression left-to-right with IEEE-754 binary64 operations; do not fuse or algebraically reorder it
psth_derived_counts.nearestInteger: round rawCount to the nearest mathematical integer; exact half ties go toward positive infinity (half ties necessarily fail the 1e-6 recovery tolerance)
psth_derived_counts.rule: count values are exact non-negative safe integers; normalized values pass only when rawCount and rounded are finite, rounded is a non-negative safe integer, and abs(rawCount-rounded) <= absoluteTolerance
psth_derived_counts.relativeTolerance: none; this constraint uses absoluteTolerance only

max_parallel_edges.pathRoles: the first path resolves an array of edges with source and target ids
max_parallel_edges.pairIdentity: source/target direction is ignored; canonicalize each pair by ECMAScript UTF-16 lexicographic order
max_parallel_edges.rule: the number of edges for every canonical unordered endpoint pair is <= max

each_unique_field.pathRoles: the first path resolves zero or more arrays of objects; field names the key
each_unique_field.rule: within each resolved array, field values are unique under JSON scalar equality

each_contains_field_value.pathRoles: the first path resolves zero or more arrays of objects; field names the key
each_contains_field_value.rule: within each resolved array, at least one object field value occurs in allowedFieldValues under JSON string equality

node_score_kind.pathRoles: the first path resolves an array of nodes with kind and optional uncalibrated_score.kind
node_score_kind.rule: a present score discriminator occurs in allowedScoreKinds[node.kind]

edge_score_kind.pathRoles: the first path resolves an array of edges with kind and optional uncalibrated_score.kind
edge_score_kind.rule: a present score discriminator occurs in allowedScoreKinds[edge.kind]; an empty allowed list forbids scores for that edge kind

ordered_interval.pathRoles: first path resolves one finite interval start; second resolves one finite interval stop
ordered_interval.rule: stop is strictly greater than start

uniform_bin_window.pathRoles: ordered bin-center array, positive finite bin width, finite window start, finite window stop in that order
uniform_bin_window.rule: centers are strictly increasing and uniformly spaced by width; firstCenter-width/2 equals start and lastCenter+width/2 equals stop
uniform_bin_window.spacingComparison: adjacent center deltas use abs(actual-expected) <= absoluteTolerance + relativeTolerance * max(abs(actual),abs(expected))
uniform_bin_window.edgeComparison: exact edge equality passes; otherwise the binary64 allowance must be <= maxRoundoffFraction * abs(binWidth), then abs(edge-expected) <= absoluteTolerance + relativeTolerance * abs(binWidth) + roundoffUlps * 2^-52 * max(abs(center),abs(binWidth/2),abs(edge),abs(expected)); an unresolved absolute origin fails closed

population_rate_derived_values.pathRoles: series array, shared bin-center array, positive finite bin width, normalization, aggregation, and binning literals in that order
population_rate_derived_values.seriesRule: series ids are unique; recorded_sender_count is a positive safe integer; spike_counts are non-negative safe integers; spike_counts and rates_hz each match the shared bin count
population_rate_derived_values.operationOrder: multiply spikeCount by 1000; multiply recordedSenderCount by binWidthMs; divide the first result by the second using IEEE-754 binary64; do not fuse or algebraically reorder

symmetric_lag_axis.pathRoles: ordered lag-center array, positive finite bin width, positive finite tau_max_ms in that order
symmetric_lag_axis.rule: lags are strictly increasing, uniformly spaced by width, odd in count, pairwise symmetric about a zero center, and span exactly [-tau_max_ms,+tau_max_ms]

legacy_connection_channels.pathRoles: optional weights array, optional weight_units, optional delays array, and optional delay_units in that order
legacy_connection_channels.rule: weights and weight_units occur together; delays and delay_units occur together; every present delay is finite and strictly positive
legacy_connection_channels.emptyChannels: a present empty measurement array still requires its matching unit

connection_graph_snapshot.pathRoles: nodes array, edges array, sample_policy, source_connection_count, optional weight_units, optional delay_units, and edge_identity in that order
connection_graph_snapshot.rule: node and edge ids are unique; every edge endpoint exists; weight, delay_ms, and synapse_model are each present on every edge or none; measurement units occur exactly with their channel; complete output has edges.length=source_connection_count; deterministic_even_stride is a non-empty strict subset
connection_graph_snapshot.identity: canonical_sorted_ordinal requires connection:<safe ordinal> with ordinal < source_connection_count; nest_connection_identifier requires connection:source:target:target_thread:synapse_id:port with canonical nonnegative safe-integer components and endpoint correlation

matrix_connection_counts.pathRoles: ordered source_ids, ordered target_ids, sparse cells, total connection_count, and aggregation in that order
matrix_connection_counts.rule: axis ids are unique; every cell has a unique in-universe source/target pair and positive safe-integer connection_count; the left-to-right safe-integer cell-count sum equals connection_count; single_connection requires every cell count to equal one
matrix_connection_counts.absence: a missing sparse cell means no_connection; a present zero-valued weight cell remains a connection because connection_count is positive

degree_distribution_consistency.pathRoles: degrees, node_counts, displayed values, node_count, connection_count, direction, normalization, value_units, edge_counting, and zero_degree_policy in that order
degree_distribution_consistency.rule: degrees equal contiguous integers 0..N; counts and nonnegative values match their length; sum(node_counts)=node_count; sum(degree*node_count)=connection_count; displayed counts equal raw counts exactly; probabilities match raw count/node_count and sum to one

delay_distribution_consistency.pathRoles: bin centers, raw delay_counts, displayed values, bin width, connection_count, normalization, value units, delay units, aggregation, and binning in that order
delay_distribution_consistency.rule: the three bin arrays have equal length; displayed values are finite and nonnegative; sum(delay_counts)=connection_count; displayed counts equal raw counts exactly; probabilities or densities exactly equal the published binary64 recovery result and globally sum or integrate to one within the accumulated-mass tolerance; non-count normalization requires a non-empty snapshot and finite density denominator
delay_distribution_consistency.operationOrder: probability=count/connection_count; probability_density=count/(connection_count*bin_width_ms) using IEEE-754 binary64; per-bin comparison uses exact Object.is-equivalent binary64 identity, while absoluteTolerance/relativeTolerance apply only to accumulated normalized mass
delay_distribution_consistency.geometry: a separate uniform_bin_window constraint publishes and evaluates exact [start,stop) bin geometry

spatial_extent_bounds.pathRoles: nodes array, extent tuple, and center tuple in that order
spatial_extent_bounds.rule: center ± extent/2 must produce a strictly ordered representable interval on each axis; node ids are unique and every coordinate lies within those bounds using a separate tolerance for each axis
spatial_extent_bounds.comparison: axisTolerance = absoluteTolerance + relativeTolerance * abs(halfExtent) + boundedRoundoff; boundedRoundoff is the roundoffUlps * 2^-52 arithmetic allowance only when it is <= maxRoundoffFraction * abs(halfExtent), otherwise zero; the large absolute spatial origin never multiplies relativeTolerance
spatial_extent_bounds.roundoff: roundoffUlps and maxRoundoffFraction bound IEEE-754 binary64 repair for deriving center ± extent/2; exact in-bound comparisons remain valid when repair is disabled

scope_compatibility.pathRoles: scope object and optional degree direction in that order
scope_compatibility.rule: rank-local scopes require integer 0<=rank<world_size; merged scopes require positive world_size; out-degree forbids mpi_target_rank_local

acyclic.pathRoles: first path resolves node ids; second resolves each node parent id or null
acyclic.rule: following parent links from any id never revisits an id

skills.title: NEST voltage trace renderer
skills.description: Render labeled multimeter/voltmeter series for one recorded variable and unit.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Declared units must match the rendered trace-axis units.

paramConstraints.description: Every trace series must have one non-empty label.
Every trace series must contain one value per times_ms sample.
Trace timestamps must be monotonically non-decreasing.

examples.dataShape: times_ms + same-unit series split and labeled by sender
examples.output: Labeled same-unit trace series over the checked millisecond axis
examples.note: Use one invocation per variable/unit; never mix mV, pA and nS on one axis.

skills.title: NEST spike raster renderer
skills.description: Render exact spike_recorder event times and sender ids as a sender-time raster.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: The times_ms axis is expressed in milliseconds.

paramConstraints.description: Every spike timestamp must have one sender id.

examples.dataShape: spike_recorder events: times_ms, senders, population labels
examples.output: Exact sender-time raster with no invented rate bins or synthetic events
examples.note: Use exact spike times first; aggregate only when too dense to read.

skills.title: NEST inter-spike interval distribution renderer
skills.description: Render an explicitly normalized histogram of within-sender or single-train inter-spike intervals.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Inter-spike interval bin centers and widths are expressed in milliseconds.
Declared bin width must match params.bin_width_ms.
Declared histogram normalization must match params.normalization.
Declared interval scope must match the rendered interval calculation.

paramConstraints.description: Every ISI histogram bin center must have one value.
ISI bin centers must be monotonically non-decreasing.
ISI bins must be strictly increasing, uniformly spaced by bin_width_ms, and have a non-negative lower edge.
ISI bin centers and histogram values cannot be negative.
Each ISI normalization has one unambiguous value unit.
ISI counts are safe integers, probabilities lie in [0,1], and density values are non-negative.
ISI probability mass must sum to one and probability density must integrate to one.

examples.dataShape: ordered ISI bin centers plus counts, probabilities, or probability density
examples.output: Inter-spike interval histogram with explicit scope and normalization
examples.note: Compute intervals within each sender; never difference a globally interleaved recorder stream.

skills.title: NEST peri-stimulus time histogram renderer
skills.description: Render trial-aligned aggregate spike counts across selected senders, counts per trial, or firing rates around a declared event.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: PSTH bin centers and widths are expressed in milliseconds.
Declared bin width must match params.bin_width_ms.
Declared histogram normalization must match params.normalization.
Declared event alignment must match params.alignment_event.
Declared PSTH aggregation must match params.aggregation.

paramConstraints.description: Every PSTH bin center must have one value.
PSTH bin centers must be monotonically non-decreasing.
PSTH bins must be strictly increasing and uniformly spaced by bin_width_ms.
PSTH values cannot be negative.
Each PSTH normalization has one unambiguous value unit.
PSTH counts are safe integers and all normalized values are non-negative.
Every displayed PSTH value must recover an integer aggregate spike-event count across the selected senders and trials.

examples.nestExample: Sinusoidal gamma generator example (one selected sender, one trial)
examples.dataShape: single-trial time bins aggregated across the selected sender set
examples.output: Peri-stimulus time histogram with auditable normalization
examples.note: The linked example is one trial; keep sender aggregation, trial count, bin width and alignment event in the checked payload.

skills.title: NEST population-rate renderer
skills.description: Render auditable mean firing-rate series derived from raw per-bin spike counts and the exact recorded-sender denominator.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Population-rate bin centers, widths, and window bounds are expressed in milliseconds.
Declared bin width must match params.bin_width_ms.
Declared rate normalization must match params.normalization.
Declared bin interval policy must match params.binning.

paramConstraints.description: Every population spike-count series has one value per shared time bin.
Every population-rate series has one value per shared time bin.
Population-rate series ids must be unique.
The population-rate counting window must have positive duration.
Uniform left-closed/right-open bins must exactly cover the declared [window_start_ms,window_stop_ms) interval.
Every mean-per-recorded-sender rate must be recoverable from its raw integer spike count, sender denominator, and bin width.

examples.nestExample: Population firing-rate trace derived from spike_recorder events
examples.dataShape: uniform [start,stop) bins, raw population spike counts, sender denominator, and derived mean rates
examples.output: One or more auditable mean-per-recorded-sender population-rate traces
examples.note: Preserve raw counts and the exact recorded sender count; never divide by an undeclared population size.

skills.title: NEST rate / IF response renderer
skills.description: Render firing-rate / IF response points against declared stimulus amplitudes.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Declared stimulus units must match params.stimulus_units.

paramConstraints.description: Every stimulus amplitude must have one firing-rate value.
Firing rates cannot be negative.

examples.dataShape: stimulus amplitudes and rates_hz with declared stimulus units
examples.output: F-I response line and points with declared stimulus and rate units
examples.note: Show the declared bin width and rate normalization; this legacy envelope carries no counting-window bounds.

skills.title: NEST connectivity edge-list topology renderer
skills.description: Render SynapseCollection endpoint pairs and optional unit-bound weight and delay channels as schematic node-link topology (legacy skill id; not a literal matrix heatmap).
skills.weakDisclosure: Schematic topology layout — node positions and distances are derived for readability; only the declared endpoint pairs and optional measurement channels are evidence.

deprecation.message: Legacy edge-list skill id; use nest.connection_graph for explicit graph, snapshot, sampling, and multapse semantics.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: When declared, legacy graph weight units must match params.weight_units.
When declared, legacy graph delay units must match params.delay_units.

paramConstraints.description: Connection endpoints and optional measurement channels are parallel arrays.
Legacy optional measurement channels remain unit-bound and delays remain strictly positive.

examples.dataShape: parallel source/target endpoint arrays plus optional unit-bound weights and delays
examples.output: Schematic node-edge topology from the checked edge list
examples.note: Optional weights and delays remain edge measurements; topology positions and distances are schematic.

skills.title: NEST connection topology graph renderer
skills.description: Render a complete or explicitly deterministic sample of a SynapseCollection snapshot while preserving isolates, autapses, multapses, and measured channels.
skills.weakDisclosure: Schematic topology layout — circle positions and distances are derived for readability; edges are complete or deterministically sampled exactly as declared.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Declared graph sampling must match params.sample_policy.
Declared snapshot time must match params.snapshot_time_ms.
Declared snapshot scope must match params.snapshot_scope.kind.
Declared parallel-edge policy must match params.parallel_edges.
When declared, graph weight units must match params.weight_units.
When declared, graph delay units must match params.delay_units.

paramConstraints.description: Graph identity, endpoint, optional-channel, unit, and sample-count semantics remain auditable.
Snapshot MPI rank metadata must be internally valid.

examples.dataShape: explicit node universe plus one preserved graph edge per selected SynapseCollection entry
examples.output: Schematic directed topology graph with disclosed completeness and snapshot scope
examples.note: Circle placement is schematic; complete and deterministic samples are never conflated.

skills.title: NEST binary adjacency matrix renderer
skills.description: Render sparse connection presence with target rows, source columns, and explicit multapse counts.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Only complete connection snapshots may form a literal matrix.
Declared snapshot time must match params.
Declared snapshot scope must match params.
Parallel connections are preserved as each sparse cell count.
Declared matrix axes must match params.
Declared matrix aggregation must match params.

paramConstraints.description: Sparse adjacency cells are unique, in-universe, positive-count entries whose counts recover the snapshot total.
Snapshot MPI rank metadata must be internally valid.

examples.dataShape: ordered source/target axes plus sparse positive connection-count cells
examples.output: Binary adjacency heatmap with target rows and source columns
examples.note: Absent cells mean no connection; multapses remain visible through connection_count.

skills.title: NEST connection-weight matrix renderer
skills.description: Render explicitly aggregated SynapseCollection weights without conflating absent and zero-valued cells.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Weight units must match params.
Only complete connection snapshots may form a literal matrix.
Snapshot time must match params.
Snapshot scope must match params.
Parallel connections remain auditable via connection_count.
Matrix axis order must match params.
Weight aggregation must match params.

paramConstraints.description: Sparse weight cells are unique, in-universe, positive-count entries whose counts recover the snapshot total.
Snapshot MPI rank metadata must be internally valid.

examples.dataShape: ordered node axes plus sparse measured-weight cells and multapse counts
examples.output: Unit-labelled weight heatmap
examples.note: A present zero/cancelled cell remains distinct from an absent connection.

skills.title: NEST synaptic-delay matrix renderer
skills.description: Render explicitly aggregated positive synaptic delays in milliseconds.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Delay units must match params.
Only complete connection snapshots may form a literal matrix.
Snapshot time must match params.
Snapshot scope must match params.
Parallel connections remain auditable via connection_count.
Matrix axis order must match params.
Delay aggregation must match params.

paramConstraints.description: Sparse delay cells are unique, in-universe, positive-count entries whose counts recover the snapshot total.
Snapshot MPI rank metadata must be internally valid.

examples.dataShape: ordered node axes plus sparse positive delay cells and multapse counts
examples.output: Millisecond delay heatmap
examples.note: Parallel-delay aggregation is always explicit.

skills.title: NEST in-degree distribution renderer
skills.description: Render the measured incoming-edge distribution over the complete declared target universe.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Degree input must be complete for its declared scope.
Snapshot time must match params.
Snapshot scope must match params.
Every multapse contributes one degree edge.
Degree direction must match params.
Degree counting must match params.
Zero-degree policy must match params.
Degree normalization must match params.

paramConstraints.description: In-degree bins, raw node counts, totals, normalization, and displayed values agree exactly.
In-degree accepts valid complete or target-rank-local snapshot scope.

examples.dataShape: contiguous degree bins, exact node counts, and explicit zero-degree inclusion
examples.output: In-degree count or probability distribution
examples.note: Each SynapseCollection entry counts, including multapses.

skills.title: NEST out-degree distribution renderer
skills.description: Render the measured outgoing-edge distribution over the complete declared source universe.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Degree input must be complete for its declared scope.
Snapshot time must match params.
Snapshot scope must match params.
Every multapse contributes one degree edge.
Degree direction must match params.
Degree counting must match params.
Zero-degree policy must match params.
Degree normalization must match params.

paramConstraints.description: Out-degree bins, raw node counts, totals, normalization, and displayed values agree exactly.
Out-degree rejects target-rank-local evidence and validates merged-rank metadata.

examples.dataShape: contiguous degree bins, exact node counts, and explicit zero-degree inclusion
examples.output: Out-degree count or probability distribution
examples.note: Target-rank-local GetConnections evidence is rejected for out-degree.

skills.title: NEST synaptic-delay distribution renderer
skills.description: Render exact half-open bins over one delay value per selected connection.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Delay units must match params.
Delay histogram input must be complete for its scope.
Snapshot time must match params.
Snapshot scope must match params.
Every selected connection contributes one delay.
Delay bin width must match params.
Delay normalization must match params.
Delay binning policy must match params.

paramConstraints.description: Uniform left-closed/right-open delay bins exactly cover the declared window.
Raw delay counts, normalization, and displayed values recover one another.
Snapshot MPI rank metadata must be internally valid.

examples.dataShape: one positive millisecond delay per selected connection in exact uniform bins
examples.output: Delay count, probability, or probability-density histogram
examples.note: Out-of-window delays are transform errors, never silently discarded.

skills.title: NEST connection-weight histogram renderer
skills.description: Render the measured weight distribution of a declared GetConnections snapshot.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Declared weight units must match params.weight_units.
Declared histogram normalization must match params.normalization.

paramConstraints.description: Every weight histogram bin center must have one value.
Weight histogram bin centers must be monotonically non-decreasing.
Weight histogram bins must be strictly increasing and uniformly spaced by bin_width.
Weight histogram values cannot be negative.
Each weight histogram normalization has one unambiguous value unit.
Weight counts are safe integers and probabilities lie in [0,1].
Weight-histogram probability mass must sum to one.

examples.nestExample: Plot weight matrices example / SynapseCollection snapshot
examples.dataShape: binned GetConnections weights at one declared simulation time
examples.output: Connection-weight count or probability histogram
examples.note: Use a GetConnections snapshot; weight_recorder events are update-event samples and bias distributions.

skills.title: NEST legacy 2D position host envelope
skills.description: Validate anonymous 2D position tuples and coordinate units for an explicitly selected host renderer; Cortexel supplies no scene.

deprecation.message: Legacy host-only coordinate list; use nest.spatial_map_2d for identified nodes and explicit layer/MPI semantics.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Declared spatial units must match the coordinate axis units.

examples.nestExample: Circular mask, Gaussian kernel, grid/free spatial examples
examples.dataShape: anonymous x/y position tuples plus coordinate units
examples.output: Validated host envelope only; the selected host owns rendering and caption display.
examples.note: Extent, mask, and kernel are caller-declared metadata, not structured render data; use nest.spatial_map_2d for identified measured positions.

skills.title: NEST measured 2D spatial map renderer
skills.description: Render identified 2D GetPosition coordinates inside the declared layer extent with explicit periodic-boundary and MPI completeness semantics.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Declared spatial units must match params.coordinate_units.
Declared position scope must match params.position_scope.kind.

paramConstraints.description: Every identified 2D node lies within the declared center ± extent/2 bounds.
Position MPI rank metadata must be internally valid.

examples.dataShape: identified x/y coordinates plus layer extent, center, edge-wrap, units, and completeness scope
examples.output: Equal-aspect measured spatial node map
examples.note: Masks and probability kernels are separate analyses and are not invented from GetPosition.

skills.title: NEST 3D spatial renderer
skills.description: Render 3D population/node positions for spatial inspection.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Declared spatial units must match the coordinate axis units.

examples.dataShape: x/y/z positioned objects plus coordinate units
examples.output: Unit-labelled 3D positioned-node scene for host rendering
examples.note: Extent and projection-sample policy are caller declarations, not edge data; use 3D only as a positioned-node inspection aid.

skills.title: NEST plasticity dynamics renderer
skills.description: Render recorded synaptic-weight samples over time.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Declared weight units must match the rendered weight axis.

paramConstraints.description: Every plasticity timestamp must have one weight value.
Plasticity timestamps must be monotonically non-decreasing.

examples.dataShape: weight-recorder times_ms and weights in one declared unit
examples.output: Measured synaptic-weight trace over time
examples.note: This contract does not contain an STDP window or pre/post spike protocol; do not invent either.

skills.title: NEST phase-plane renderer
skills.description: Render a checked Cartesian phase-plane vector field.

paramConstraints.description: A phase plane has exactly two state-variable axes.
Derivative fields must use the same state-variable names as the grid.
Every phase-plane axis must declare its coordinate units.
Every phase-plane derivative field must declare its units.
Each derivative field has one value per Cartesian grid point.
axis_order must contain every grid key exactly once, in flattening order.

declared_inputs.derivation_method: model equations evaluated on Cartesian grid
declared_inputs.fixed_parameters: all non-plotted state variables clamped to declared values

examples.nestExample: Numerical phase-plane analysis of the Hodgkin-Huxley neuron
examples.dataShape: state-variable axes plus flattened derivative arrays and explicit ordering
examples.output: Unit-labelled phase-plane vector field
examples.note: No nullcline, trajectory, or equilibrium is present in this contract; do not invent one.

skills.title: NEST correlogram / synchrony renderer
skills.description: Render a symmetric correlation_detector lag histogram with explicit pair orientation, interval policy, counting window, zero-lag handling, and statistic semantics.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Declared bin width must match params.bin_width_ms.
Declared reference population must match params.pair.reference_label.
Declared target population must match params.pair.target_label.
Declared normalization/statistic must match params.statistic.kind.
Declared value units must match params.statistic.units.
Declared lag convention must match params.lag_convention.
Declared bin interval policy must match params.binning.

paramConstraints.description: Every lag must have one correlogram value.
Correlogram lag centers must be strictly increasing, uniform, odd, zero-centered, symmetric, and span [-tau_max_ms,+tau_max_ms].
The correlogram counting window must have positive duration.
Correlogram values must obey the numeric domain implied by their discriminated statistic.

examples.nestExample: Auto- and crosscorrelation functions for spike trains
examples.dataShape: symmetric lag centers, values, bin/tau/counting-window semantics, oriented population pair, and discriminated statistic
examples.output: Canonical correlogram distinct from ISI and other time histograms
examples.note: Positive lag means the target population spikes after the reference population; never infer orientation from a display label.

skills.title: NEST stimulus-response host envelope
skills.description: Validate aligned time, stimulus, and response arrays for an explicitly selected host renderer; Cortexel supplies no scene.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: The times_ms axis is expressed in milliseconds.

paramConstraints.description: Time, stimulus, and response samples must align one-to-one.
Stimulus-response timestamps must be monotonically non-decreasing.

examples.nestExample: Sinusoidal generator / pulse packet / repeated stimulation
examples.dataShape: aligned times_ms, stimulus, and response arrays
examples.output: Validated host envelope only; the selected host owns any composite panels.
examples.note: The envelope carries no spike-event or epoch structure; the host must not infer either.

skills.title: NEST astrocyte concentration-trace renderer
skills.description: Render one declared non-negative glial concentration trace carried as ca_trace.
skills.weakDisclosure: Derived view — a declared glial concentration trace is shown through the analog-trace scene; it is not membrane voltage.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: Declared units must match the rendered glial trace units.
The times_ms axis is expressed in milliseconds.

paramConstraints.description: Every glial sample must have one timestamp.
Glial timestamps must be monotonically non-decreasing.
The declared Ca²⁺ concentration trace cannot be negative.

examples.dataShape: times_ms, one ca_trace array, and its declared units
examples.output: One glial concentration trace via the analog-trace scene (flagged derived)
examples.note: The legacy envelope carries neither multiple state variables nor linked neuronal events.

skills.title: NEST compartment-tree trace host envelope
skills.description: Validate an id/parent compartment topology and aligned per-compartment values for an explicitly selected host renderer.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: The times_ms axis is expressed in milliseconds.

paramConstraints.description: Every compartment trace has one value per timestamp.
Compartment ids must be unique.
Every non-null parent id must reference a declared compartment.
The compartment parent graph must be acyclic.
Compartment timestamps must be monotonically non-decreasing.

examples.dataShape: times_ms plus compartments with id, parent_id, optional label, and aligned values
examples.output: Validated host envelope for a schematic compartment tree and aligned traces.
examples.note: The envelope carries no receptor-port or morphology-geometry data; the host must not invent either.

skills.title: NEST state replay / animation storyboard renderer
skills.description: Render time-evolution storyboards and inspectable state replays.

paramConstraints.description: Replay frame timestamps must be monotonically non-decreasing.
Every replay frame state must contain at least one field.

examples.dataShape: frames, entities, metrics, frame rate, annotations
examples.output: Manim storyboard / source — no live Cortexel scene.
examples.note: scene:null — offline storyboard, not a real-time render target.

skills.title: Corpus knowledge-graph 3D renderer
skills.description: Render a bounded, traceable cross-paper entity multigraph: paper/model/family nodes plus identified citation, instantiation, family and advisory identity assertions. Every element carries typed source evidence; every numeric score is discriminated and explicitly uncalibrated.
skills.weakDisclosure: Advisory graph — every corpus-entity assertion is derived; same_as/variant_of edges are structural similarity, not certified sameness; force-layout positions and distances are schematic, not quantitative evidence.

provenanceParamConstraints.description: The declared graph source must match params.graph_source.
The declared immutable snapshot must match params.graph_snapshot_id.
The declared graph scope must match params.graph_scope.
Corpus identity and genealogy assertions are always advisory.

paramConstraints.description: Node ids must be unique.
Edge assertion ids must be unique; parallel assertions remain distinct by id.
At most nine identified assertions may connect one unordered node pair.
Every edge endpoint must reference a declared node id.
Self-loop edges are not renderable by this scene.
Node attribute maps are bounded.
Edge attribute maps are bounded.
Evidence ids must be unique within each node evidence bundle.
Every node evidence bundle must contain a direct source anchor; graph_node references may only supplement it.
Evidence ids must be unique within each edge evidence bundle.
Every edge evidence bundle must contain a direct source anchor; graph_node references may only supplement it.
Every graph_node evidence reference on a node must resolve.
Every graph_node evidence reference on an edge must resolve.
An optional node score may only report extraction confidence; other quantities lack a node-kind context.
An optional edge score must state the quantity that edge kind actually computes.
Each semantic edge kind has a fixed source-kind and target-kind contract.

examples.nestExample: Cross-paper corpus knowledge graph (papers + models + families)
examples.dataShape: snapshot-bound paper/model/family nodes and stable-id multigraph edges, each with typed evidence, bounded attributes, derived/advisory epistemic status and optional uncalibrated scores
examples.output: Traceable 3D force-directed multigraph with citation-flow particles and accessible evidence detail
examples.note: 1.4 contract: every assertion is traceable; identity edges are advisory and force-layout geometry is non-evidentiary.
